<task-notification>
<task-id>a5cbe55e323a4a24c</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_014YJXTFhepy6ajZP8p1cjzZ</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\a5cbe55e323a4a24c.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 1" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Now I have everything I need. Here is the review.

---

## 1. Spec Compliance: ‚úÖ

**Files required vs. delivered:**

| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| `loadRun.ts`, `sliceSpec.ts`, `types.ts` | ‚úÖ All created |
| `__tests__/sliceSpec.test.ts` | ‚úÖ Created |
| `loadRun(path): RunConfig` | ‚úÖ `loadRun.ts:4‚Äì5` |
| `buildSliceSpecs(run, backlogPath): SliceSpec[]` | ‚úÖ `sliceSpec.ts:115` |
| `SliceSpec = { id, title, prompt }` | ‚úÖ `types.ts:43‚Äì46` |
| Prompt contains title + rationale + ACs | ‚úÖ `buildPrompt` includes both |
| Explicit instructions: worktree / write tests / commit / never block | ‚úÖ `sliceSpec.ts:81‚Äì85` |
| Touch-set as hints, not commands | ‚úÖ "Hint files (touch-set)" label, `sliceSpec.ts:105` |
| Test: 3 specs with S1/S2/S3 IDs | ‚úÖ `sliceSpec.test.ts:46‚Äì84` |
| **Information asymmetry ‚Äî both directions** | ‚úÖ fully covered (see below) |

**Asymmetry coverage in tests:**

The brief required bidirectional tests. What's present:
- S1 contains F-08 AC ("viewer-role JWT") ‚Äî `test.ts:87‚Äì93`
- S1 NOT F-06 ("microscope") ‚Äî `test.ts:103‚Äì109`
- S1 NOT F-17 ("text/csv") ‚Äî `test.ts:111‚Äì117`
- S2 contains F-06 ("microscope") ‚Äî `test.ts:119‚Äì124`
- S3 contains F-17 ("text/csv") ‚Äî `test.ts:126‚Äì131`
- S2 NOT F-08 ("requireRole") ‚Äî `test.ts:133‚Äì139`
- S3 NOT F-06 ("microscope") ‚Äî `test.ts:141‚Äì146`

Both directions for S1 (the brief's stated requirement) are covered. The additional S2/S3 exclusion tests are a bonus but the matrix has two gaps (S2 not S3, S3 not S1) ‚Äî not required by the brief, so not a deficiency.

**No YAGNI violations in behavior** ‚Äî no extra flags, no runtime abstractions beyond the brief.

---

## 2. Code Quality: Approved ‚Äî with findings

### Minor

**`sliceSpec.ts:8` ‚Äî dead `sectionPattern` variable.**
```ts
const sectionPattern = /^### `([A-Z]+-\d+)` ‚Äî (.+)$/m;
```
Defined at line 1468 of the diff, never referenced. The actual match at line 1473 uses a separate inline literal. This is dead code and a reader distraction. Harmless but should be removed. TypeScript won't catch it without `"noUnusedLocals": true` in tsconfig.

**`types.ts:15‚Äì40` ‚Äî speculative typing of full `RunConfig`.**
Fields like `arm`, `topology`, `gate`, `integration`, `trials`, `metrics` are not accessed by task 1. Modeling the full YAML schema upfront has value for the project but is technically beyond the task scope. Acceptable here given the sub-project context; call it out for awareness.

**`sliceSpec.test.ts:90, 98, 134, 143` ‚Äî bare `!` non-null assertions.**
```ts
const s1 = specs.find(s => s.id === "S1")!;
expect(s1.prompt).toContain("viewer-role JWT");
```
If `find` returns `undefined` (e.g., a regression makes S1 disappear), this produces `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined` rather than a clean test failure. The prior test asserting three correct IDs reduces the risk, but a `expect(s1).toBeDefined()` guard before `.prompt` access is cleaner.

### Important (robustness)

**`sliceSpec.ts:52` ‚Äî silent AC extraction failure.**
```ts
const acMatch = part.match(
  /\*\*Acceptance criteria \(behavioral ‚Äî gate material\):\*\*\n((?:\s+-\s+.+\n?)+)/
);
```
The literal em-dash `‚Äî` (U+2014) must match exactly. If the backlog header text changes (even slightly ‚Äî e.g., casing, wording, or encoding artifact), `acMatch` is `null` and `acceptanceCriteria` is silently `[]`. No error is thrown, and a prompt built with empty ACs still passes the asymmetry tests (which check for presence of specific strings from the *actual* backlog content). The report acknowledges format brittleness but doesn't flag the silent failure mode. At minimum a warning log or a post-parse assert (`if (acceptanceCriteria.length === 0) throw`) would surface the problem without requiring a manual diff.

---

## 3. Cannot verify from diff

- **`benchmark/runs/standard-L1.run.yaml` and `benchmark/backlog/backlog.md`** ‚Äî the tests resolve these at runtime against the actual repo files. I cannot confirm from the diff alone that those files have the exact strings the tests assert (`viewer-role JWT`, `requireRole`, `microscope`, `text/csv`). The 19-pass report from `pnpm test` is the evidence; trust it unless the run config or backlog was modified after the test was written.
- **`loadRun` type safety at runtime** ‚Äî `parse(raw) as RunConfig` (loadRun.ts:5) is an unchecked cast; the types assume the YAML matches the schema. No runtime validation exists. This will produce confusing errors if the YAML is missing a required field, but it is outside the stated scope of task 1.

---

**Bottom line:** Spec is fully met. The dead `sectionPattern` variable and silent AC-parse failure on header mismatch are the two things worth fixing before downstream tasks rely on this; everything else is minor style. The asymmetry tests are substantive and would genuinely fail without the isolation logic.</result>

author: JB
text:
<task-notification>
<task-id>aa0a34e65ee372ccf</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_019Vs1bwuxp1MEgzBxJkxvh5</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\aa0a34e65ee372ccf.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 2" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## 1. Spec compliance

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Create `benchmark/runner/workspace.ts` | ‚úÖ | diff: new file, 83 lines |
| Create `benchmark/runner/__tests__/workspace.test.ts` | ‚úÖ | diff: new file, 118 lines |
| `provision(run)` ‚Äî one worktree per slice on new branch `run/<runId>/<sliceId>` off `arena/base` | ‚úÖ | workspace.ts:199 |
| `pnpm install` in each worktree | ‚úÖ | workspace.ts:201-203, gated by `install` opt (false in tests) |
| Returns `Workspace[]` with paths + branch names | ‚úÖ | workspace.ts:156-160, 205 |
| `teardown(workspaces)` | ‚úÖ | workspace.ts:215-233 |
| Test: N slices ‚Üí N distinct dirs, each on its own branch off `arena/base` | ‚úÖ | test:76-88 (dirs), test:90-100 (SHA check) |
| Test: teardown removes them and prunes | ‚úÖ | test:102-116 |
| Test: re-provision with same runId is clean | ‚úÖ | test:118-133 |

**Extra (not in brief, not harmful):** Double-teardown idempotency test (test:135-143), `.gitignore` addition.

**Missing:** Nothing from the brief is absent.

---

## 2. Code quality: Approved ‚Äî with findings

### Important

**workspace.ts:152 ‚Äî `execSync` used for `pnpm install`, inconsistent with `execFileSync` everywhere else.**
`execSync("pnpm install", ...)` goes through the shell; the rest of the file uses `execFileSync`. With a hardcoded string there is no injection risk, but the inconsistency is gratuitous. Use `execFileSync("pnpm", ["install"], { cwd: dir, stdio: "inherit" })`.

**test:118-133 ‚Äî `ws1` is outside the `afterEach` guard.**
The idempotency test provisions `ws1` and assigns it to a local (not to `workspaces`). If `teardown(ws1)` throws unexpectedly, those 3 worktrees and branches are not cleaned up by `afterEach` because `workspaces` is still `[]` at the point of failure. Fix: assign `ws1` to `workspaces` immediately after provision and clear it only after the manual teardown succeeds:

```ts
workspaces = await provision(run, { rootDir: tmpDir, install: false });
await teardown(workspaces);
workspaces = []; // now safe
workspaces = await provision(run, { rootDir: tmpDir2, install: false });
```

### Minor

**test:71 ‚Äî `afterEach` swallows all teardown errors silently.**
`.catch(() => {})` means a teardown that fails for an unexpected reason (not "already removed") leaves stray git state without any diagnostic. At minimum: `.catch(e => console.error("[afterEach] teardown failed:", e))`. This does not affect correctness of passing tests but obscures leaks during development.

**test:125 ‚Äî `tmpDir2` is not cleaned up.**
Not a git leak, but the OS temp dir is left behind. Low severity.

**workspace.ts:167,169 ‚Äî `REPO_ROOT` / `DEFAULT_WORK_DIR` fragile under compilation.**
Both are resolved from `__dirname` at runtime. In the Vitest/tsx context `__dirname` = `benchmark/runner` and both resolve correctly. If the source is ever compiled to `dist/`, `path.resolve(__dirname, "../..")` resolves to `benchmark/` (not repo root). Works today; would silently break on a compile-and-run path. Add a comment noting this assumption.

---

## 3. Cross-task / cannot verify from diff

**`teardown` permanently destroys slice branches (`git branch -D run/<runId>/<sliceId>`).** This is required for idempotency (re-provision with the same `runId`), and the implementation is correct in isolation. However, it destroys all agent-committed work on those branches. **The controller must invoke scoring/merge before calling `teardown`** ‚Äî if it calls `teardown` first, the benchmark output is gone. This is a cross-task ordering constraint that Task 2 cannot enforce itself; it must be documented as a precondition for the controller (Task 3 or equivalent). Flag this in the controller's brief.

**`arena/base` must exist in the repo.** Tests fail at the first `git worktree add` if it does not. Verified correct in the implementer's passing run, but not visible in this diff.

**`standard-L1.run.yaml` slice count.** Tests assert `toHaveLength(3)`; the slice count is drawn from the run config (Task 1 territory). Correct per the report but unverifiable here.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>24948</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>109364</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>**Status:** DONE

**Commit:** `7ebb333` on `feat/benchmark-runner`

**Test summary:** 37 passed (12 new + 25 existing) ‚Äî `pnpm test` green in ~2.4s, no real `claude` process or git worktree touched.

**Key decisions:**
- `Budget` interface includes `model: string` (otherwise the default `RunClaudeFn` has no model to pass to claude)
- `deps` extended to `{ runClaude?, checkCommitted? }` ‚Äî injecting the committed-check is cleaner than provisioning real worktrees in tests
- `didNotComplete` fires on any of: `exit !== 0`, `is_error` in JSON, `turns >= cap`, `tokens > cap`, or `!committed`
- JSON parsing fully defensive with `typeof` guards + try/catch; missing fields ‚Üí 0, never throws

**Concerns:**
1. `claude -p --output-format json` field names (`num_turns`, `usage.input_tokens`, etc.) are inferred from the spec ‚Äî should be validated against a live run before trusting production numbers
2. On Windows `spawn("claude")` may need `{ shell: true }` if claude ships as a `.cmd` wrapper ‚Äî the `error` handler returns `exit:1` rather than throwing, so it degrades gracefully</result>

text:
<task-notification>
<task-id>af7689ad2ec501cdf</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_0192bY9CZyhkvQY9BCm8CBi2</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\af7689ad2ec501cdf.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 3" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## 1. Spec Compliance

| Requirement | Evidence | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `runAgent(workspace, spec, budget, deps?)` signature | `agent.ts:419` ‚Äî exact match | ‚úÖ |
| `AgentResult` has `sliceId, committed, tokens, turns, wallMs, exit` | `agent.ts:315‚Äì322` | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete` field (implied by brief's test list: "did-not-complete flag") | `agent.ts:322` | ‚úÖ |
| Spawns `claude -p` with pre-granted permissions (`--dangerously-skip-permissions`) | `agent.ts:364‚Äì373` | ‚úÖ |
| Budget cap enforced (`--max-turns`) | `agent.ts:371` | ‚úÖ |
| `committed` true iff HEAD ‚âÝ `arena/base` | `agent.ts:398‚Äì409` | ‚úÖ |
| Tests use injected `runClaude` and `checkCommitted` ‚Äî no real `claude` or `git` | `agent.test.ts:66‚Äì78`; every `describe` block passes `fakeRun`/`fakeCommitted` | ‚úÖ |
| Defensive JSON parsing: never throws, missing ‚Üí 0 | `agent.ts:441‚Äì452` (`try/catch` + `typeof` guards) | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete`: `exit‚âÝ0` | `agent.ts:457` | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete`: `is_error` | `agent.ts:458` | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete`: `turns >= cap` (equality included) | `agent.ts:459` | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete`: `tokens > cap` (strict greater-than) | `agent.ts:460` | ‚úÖ |
| `didNotComplete`: `!committed` | `agent.ts:461` | ‚úÖ |
| `runAgents` via `Promise.all`, preserves input order | `agent.ts:482‚Äì485` | ‚úÖ |
| Tests: committed run ‚Üí `committed:true`, parsed tokens/turns | `agent.test.ts:85‚Äì98` | ‚úÖ |
| Tests: budget exceeded ‚Üí `didNotComplete:true` | `agent.test.ts:110‚Äì130` | ‚úÖ |
| Tests: 3 agents concurrent | `agent.test.ts:224‚Äì253` | ‚úÖ |

**Missing from brief:** Nothing missing.
**Extra beyond brief:** `didNotComplete` on `AgentResult` (correct addition). `Budget.model` field (reasonable ‚Äî default `RunClaudeFn` needs it). Three additional tests beyond the brief's list (`empty-JSON defensive`, `argv pass-through check`, `partial-failure isolation`) ‚Äî all high-quality, no dead weight.

---

## 2. Code Quality: Approved ‚Äî with one Important flag

### Important

**Windows: `spawn("claude")` will silently fail on every live run ‚Äî `agent.ts:363`**

On Windows, `claude` installed via npm is a `.cmd` wrapper. `child_process.spawn("claude", ...)` without `{ shell: true }` fires `ENOENT`; the `error` handler at `agent.ts:386‚Äì388` catches it and resolves `{ stdout: "", exit: 1 }`. Every live run on Windows will therefore return `exit:1, didNotComplete:true` with no indication that spawn itself failed ‚Äî not a parse failure, not a claude error. The report raises this but leaves it unfixed. When the live-run task arrives, callers on Windows will need to override `runClaude` or add `{ shell: true }` to the default. The current code is not broken for CI-as-Linux, but the silent degradation path is dangerous: a Windows developer running the benchmark gets all `didNotComplete` results that look like agent failures rather than an environment misconfiguration.

### Minor

**`defaultCheckCommitted` blocks the event loop ‚Äî `agent.ts:397‚Äì409`**

`execFileSync` is synchronous inside an `async` function. When N agents finish nearly simultaneously, their `checkCommitted` calls run serially on the main thread. At N=3 this is probably a few tens of milliseconds; at larger concurrency it serializes git calls that could otherwise run concurrently. The report documents this as deliberate simplicity. Flag for the live-run task.

**No assertion that `checkCommitted` receives the correct `workspace` ‚Äî `agent.test.ts:85‚Äì191`**

The `fakeCommitted` helper ignores its argument (`_ws`). No test verifies that the `workspace` object passed to `checkCommitted` is the one belonging to the current slice, not a cross-contaminated one. Low risk given the simple pass-through at `agent.ts:454`, but the injection contract is only half-tested.

**Beyond-brief test at `agent.test.ts:256‚Äì279`**

"Returns independent results even when some agents fail" ‚Äî not in the brief's test list but tests a real property (`Promise.all` doesn't reject-short-circuit because `runAgent` never rejects). Useful, not bloat.

---

## 3. Cannot Verify from Diff

- **Real `claude -p --output-format json` field names.** The parser targets `num_turns`, `usage.input_tokens`, `usage.output_tokens`, `is_error`. If a live run emits different names (e.g., `total_turns`, `inputTokens`), all values will silently be 0. Must verify against an actual `claude` invocation before trusting benchmark numbers.
- **Windows `.cmd` spawn behavior** ‚Äî structural concern analysed above; only observable on a Windows live run.
- **`arena/base` ref existence in real worktrees.** `defaultCheckCommitted` returns `false` on any git error, so a missing ref reads as `committed:false, didNotComplete:true` rather than an exception. Correct behavior, but needs the ref to be established before benchmarks run.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>28795</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>145158</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<task-notification>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 4" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## Review: Task 4 ‚Äî `mergeSlices` / mechanical merge + conflict capture

---

### 1. Spec Compliance

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Files created: `merge.ts` + `merge.test.ts` | ‚úÖ | diff header |
| Integration worktree on `run/<runId>/integration` from `arena/base` | ‚úÖ | merge.ts:301‚Äì304 |
| `git merge --no-ff` each slice in order | ‚úÖ | merge.ts:317‚Äì321 |
| No resolver; abort on conflict; continue | ‚úÖ | merge.ts:328‚Äì344 |
| `mergedCleanly` = ALL slices merged | ‚úÖ | merge.ts:349 ‚Äî `results.every((r) => r.merged)` |
| Returns `integration` as `Workspace { sliceId:"integration", dir, branch }` | ‚úÖ | merge.ts:306‚Äì310 |
| No teardown inside `mergeSlices` | ‚úÖ | confirmed; JSDoc says so explicitly at merge.ts:292 |
| Test verifies worktree is clean (no `MERGE_HEAD`) after conflict | ‚úÖ | merge.test.ts:191‚Äì193 ‚Äî `mergeInProgress()` check |
| `git ls-files --unmerged` instead of `--diff-filter=U` | ‚úÖ acceptable improvement | Catches add/add conflicts correctly; deduped via `Set` (merge.ts:272‚Äì282) |
| `afterEach` removes fixture branches even on failure | ‚úÖ | merge.test.ts:116‚Äì122 ‚Äî `git branch -D` in afterEach |
| `afterEach` removes integration worktree even on failure | ‚úÖ | merge.test.ts:110‚Äì115 ‚Äî `teardown()` in afterEach |

**Brief field-name deviation:** Brief says `{ integrationBranch, textualConflicts, mergedCleanly }`; implementation returns `{ integration: Workspace, results: MergeSliceResult[], mergedCleanly }`. The binding constraint explicitly requires `Workspace` and is authoritative. No issue. `textualConflicts` is represented as `results[].conflictedFiles` (from which count is `.length`).

**Missing:** Brief says "continue **per config**", implying a stop-on-first-conflict option may be needed. The implementation always continues; no `opts` field for this. Probably YAGNI at this task scope, but flag it.

**Extra (YAGNI):**
- `REPO_ROOT` and `DEFAULT_WORK_DIR` module-level constants (merge.ts:254‚Äì257) ‚Äî never used in tests (tests always pass `rootDir`). Harmless.
- `-c user.email=runner@clair -c user.name=Runner` on merge (merge.ts:318‚Äì320) ‚Äî not in brief, but defensively correct for headless CI.

---

### 2. Code Quality ‚Äî Needs work

**Important**

- **merge.test.ts:131‚Äì132, 169‚Äì170, 204‚Äì205 ‚Äî fixture branches registered AFTER creation.** The pattern is:
  ```ts
  createFixtureBranch(b1, ...);
  createFixtureBranch(b2, ...);
  fixtureBranches.push(b1, b2);   // ‚Üê too late
  ```
  If `createFixtureBranch(b1)` succeeds but `createFixtureBranch(b2)` throws, `b1` is never pushed to `fixtureBranches`, so afterEach never deletes it ‚Äî repo pollution. Fix: push each branch immediately after its creation call, or inside `createFixtureBranch`.

- **merge.test.ts:65‚Äì79 ‚Äî stale worktree registration on fixture commit failure.** `git worktree remove --force tempDir` is inside the `try` body, not the `finally`. If the `commit` throws, `finally` runs `fs.rmSync(tempDir)` (deletes the directory) but does NOT call `git worktree remove`, leaving a dangling worktree entry in the main repo. Subsequent test runs that hit the same tempDir path via mkdtemp collision (unlikely but not impossible) may fail weirdly. Move worktree removal into `finally`.

**Minor**

- **merge.ts:333‚Äì336 ‚Äî misleading swallow of `merge --abort` failure.** The inner `catch` comment says "Already clean ‚Äî ignore." That's one possible reason, but disk I/O errors or lock files would also land here silently, leaving the worktree dirty while the code records `merged: false` and continues. At minimum, re-throw if the abort itself is due to something other than "no merge in progress" (exit code 128 with specific message). Or log a warning.

- **merge.ts:259‚Äì261 ‚Äî `git()` void, stdout silently dropped.** Only called for worktree add, so in practice fine. But if worktree add prints a warning that indicates partial success, it's invisible. Low risk here.

---

### 3. ‚öÝÔ∏è Cannot verify from diff

- **`teardown()` branch deletion** (`benchmark/runner/workspace.js` not in diff): afterEach calls `teardown([integrationWorkspace])`. If `teardown` only removes the worktree but not the `run/<runId>/integration` branch, integration branches accumulate across test runs. Unique `runId` (timestamp + random) prevents collisions, but the branches are never cleaned. Verify `teardown` deletes the branch, or add an explicit `git branch -D integrationBranch` in afterEach.

- **`RunConfig` type shape** (`types.ts` not in diff): `makeRun` (merge.test.ts:42‚Äì57) fills many fields with empty strings/zeros. If any required field is used by `mergeSlices` indirectly (e.g., through `run.base.sha`), the test might pass vacuously. `run.base.branch` is not actually read ‚Äî the branch is hardcoded as `"arena/base"` in merge.ts:304. This is fine as long as the spec intends the base to always be `arena/base`, but confirms the `run.base.branch` field is dead in this function.

---

**Bottom line:** The core logic is correct and the binding constraints are satisfied. Two Important hygiene bugs in test fixtures (branch registration order; stale worktree on fixture failure) should be fixed before merge. The `teardown` branch-cleanup question needs a one-line check in `workspace.ts`.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>26645</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>137671</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## Review: Task 5 ‚Äî Gate Execution on the Merged Result

---

## 1. Spec Compliance

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `runGate(integration, run, deps?)` signature | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:469` |
| Gate files fetched from `origin/arena/reference` | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:480-486` ‚Äî correct argv, correct cwd |
| L1 ‚Üí `gate/acceptance.test.ts` only | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:389`, verified by test at `gate.test.ts:257-278` |
| `--reporter=json` on vitest call | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:499` |
| `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm build` floors, independent of behavioral pass | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:504-509` |
| Returns `{ perSlice, allPass, tscClean, buildClean }` | ‚úÖ | `gate.ts:545` |
| All tests use injected `runCmd` ‚Äî no real processes | ‚úÖ | All tests route through `makeFakeRunCmd` or `trackingRunCmd` |
| Brief test: all-pass ‚Üí `allPass:true` | ‚úÖ | Test 1 |
| Brief test: S2 fail ‚Üí `allPass:false` | ‚úÖ | Test 2 |
| Brief test: type-skew ‚Üí `tscClean:false` | ‚úÖ | Test 3 |
| **Slice with zero assertions NOT silently 'pass'** | ‚ùå | See Critical finding below |
| **Unknown level does not silently over-pass** | ‚ùå | See Important finding below |

---

## 2. Code Quality: Needs Work

### Critical

**`gate.ts:519` ‚Äî Default-to-pass for slices with zero parsed assertions.**

```ts
for (const s of run.slices) perSlice[s.id] = "pass";   // line 519
```

All slices are initialized to `"pass"`. The parser then only flips slices to `"fail"` when a `status !== "passed"` assertion is found for that slice ordinal. Consequence: if the vitest JSON is structurally valid but contains no assertion matching `slice N` for a given slice (regex miss on `ancestorTitles`, test file doesn't exercise that slice, misnamed describe block), that slice is silently reported `"pass"` ‚Äî a false pass on a broken or untested merge.

The brief binding constraint explicitly calls this out: *"a slice with zero assertions found (parse miss) is not silently reported 'pass'."* The implementation fails it.

Fix: track which slice ordinals have at least one assertion seen, default unmatched slices to `"fail"`.

```ts
const slicesSeen = new Set<number>();
// ... inside parse loop, on every assertion (pass or fail):
if (ord !== null) slicesSeen.add(ord);
// ... after parse loop:
for (const s of run.slices) {
  const ord = run.slices.indexOf(s) + 1;
  if (!slicesSeen.has(ord)) perSlice[s.id] = "fail";
}
```

There is also **no test** for this scenario ‚Äî a parseable JSON that simply has no assertions for S2 (e.g., `"slice 2"` was never present in `ancestorTitles`). That test gap is what let the defect through.

### Important

**`gate.ts:492` ‚Äî Silent fallback for unknown level runs L1 gate and over-reports pass.**

```ts
const gateFiles = LEVEL_GATE_FILES[run.level] ?? LEVEL_GATE_FILES["L1"];
```

If `run.level` is `"L3"`, `"L2.5"`, or any misconfiguration, this silently runs only the L1 gate. An L2 run that silently executes only the L1 acceptance file would under-test real behavior and return `allPass:true` when L2 requirements were not exercised. The report calls this "YAGNI" but this is not a future-proofing concern ‚Äî it is a correctness hazard for any caller that passes a level string not yet in the map.

The correct behavior is to throw:
```ts
const gateFiles = LEVEL_GATE_FILES[run.level];
if (!gateFiles) throw new Error(`Unknown level: "${run.level}" ‚Äî add it to LEVEL_GATE_FILES`);
```

### Minor

**`gate.test.ts:33-54` ‚Äî `makeRun` fixture hardcodes `model: "claude-opus-4-8"`.** Not harmful, but a fixture string for a model ID couples the test to an implementation detail that has nothing to do with gate execution. Use `"test-model"` or any neutral string.

---

## 3. Cannot Verify from Diff

- **Real vitest JSON shape:** The `assertionResults[].ancestorTitles` field is stated to be stable through vitest v2. Cannot confirm from diff alone; the fixture matches documented vitest JSON reporter output but live verification is out of scope.
- **`git checkout origin/arena/reference -- gate vitest.gate.config.ts` in a real merged worktree:** Whether `origin/arena/reference` is always a fetch-visible remote ref in the integration worktree context is a live-env concern; the implementation assumes it is.
- **`pnpm typecheck` as the tsc alias:** The implementation calls `pnpm typecheck` (not `tsc --noEmit` directly). Whether the target repo's `package.json` wires `typecheck` to `tsc --noEmit` cannot be confirmed from this diff.

---

**Summary:** Two defects block approval. The Critical one (default-to-pass on parse miss) is exactly the failure mode the spec asks reviewers to catch, and no test covers it. The Important one (silent unknown-level fallback) is acknowledged in the report but dismissed incorrectly as YAGNI ‚Äî it is a live correctness hazard. Fix both before merging.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>30202</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>81145</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<summary>Agent "Re-review Task 5 fix" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>**Finding 1 ‚Äî Parse-miss slice silently passes: Resolved ‚úÖ**

`gate.ts` lines 162/169-174: `slicesSeen` is populated inside the outer `if (ord !== null)` block, unconditionally ‚Äî both passing and failing assertions add their ordinal. The post-loop at lines 186-191 then marks any ordinal absent from `slicesSeen` as "fail". The catch path still marks everything "fail". No path leaves an unseen slice as "pass".

Test at `gate.test.ts` line 90-100: `SLICE2_MISSING_JSON` has assertions for ordinals 1 and 3 only. Test asserts `S1="pass"`, `S2="fail"`, `S3="pass"`, `allPass=false`. Exact match to the requirement.

**Finding 2 ‚Äî Unknown level falls back silently to L1: Resolved ‚úÖ**

`gate.ts` lines 135-140: `?? LEVEL_GATE_FILES["L1"]` removed; replaced with an explicit `throw new Error(\`Unknown level: "${run.level}" ‚Äî ...\`)`. The error message embeds the level string.

Test at `gate.test.ts` lines 103-108: `makeRun({ level: "L99" })` ‚Üí `rejects.toThrow("L99")`. The error message `Unknown level: "L99" ‚Äî add it to LEVEL_GATE_FILES` contains "L99". ‚úÖ

**Previously-passing tests unchanged?**

The only edit to existing test code is `model: "claude-opus-4-8"` ‚Üí `model: "test-model"` in `makeRun` (line 28). No existing assertion touches the model field; this is cosmetic and harmless. All other test bodies are unmodified.

**New issues introduced:** None. The throw is positioned before the `runCmd` vitest invocation, so no wasted subprocess on an unknown level.</result>

<result>## Review: Task 6 ‚Äî `writeReport`

---

## 1. Spec Compliance

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `writeReport(runId, parts, opts?)` signature | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:436‚Äì445` |
| Returns `{ json, summary, path }` | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:543` |
| `semanticConflict = mergedCleanly && !allPass` (exactly) | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:494` |
| `outcome = 'all-pass'` iff `allPass && tscClean && buildClean` else `'fail'` | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:495‚Äì498` |
| `totals.tokens/turns` summed across agents | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:487‚Äì488` |
| `agentsDidNotComplete` counts `didNotComplete:true` | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:489` |
| Summary: outcome | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:521‚Äì524` ‚Äî `"all-pass ‚úì"` contains substring "all-pass" |
| Summary: textual-conflict count | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:529` |
| Summary: SEMANTIC CONFLICT line when flag is true | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:526‚Äì528` ‚Äî `"‚öÝ SEMANTIC CONFLICT: merged clean but gate failed"` |
| Summary: did-not-complete count | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:530` |
| Summary: totals (tokens + wall-clock) | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:531` |
| `out/` git-ignored | ‚úÖ | `.gitignore:4` |
| File written to returned path | ‚úÖ | `report.ts:540‚Äì541, 543` |
| Tests use temp dir + cleanup | ‚úÖ | `report.test.ts:103‚Äì117` |
| Semantic-case test asserts `"SEMANTIC CONFLICT"` and `"merged clean but gate failed"` | ‚úÖ | `report.test.ts:208‚Äì209` |
| No assert-nothing tests | ‚úÖ | All 6 tests carry substantive assertions |
| Nothing beyond the brief | ‚úÖ | No scope creep |

**Missing:** None.
**Extra:** None.

---

## 2. Code Quality: **Approved with one Important finding**

### Important ‚Äî `report.ts:456‚Äì472` vs `report.ts:479‚Äì482`: two sub-structures built from different source sets

The `perSlice` record is driven by `parts.agents`:

```ts
for (const agent of parts.agents) {          // agents-driven
  const mr = mergeMap.get(agent.sliceId);    // merge lookup
  const gateVerdict = parts.gate.perSlice[agent.sliceId] ?? "fail";
  perSlice[agent.sliceId] = ...
}
```

But `textualConflicts.perSlice` is driven by `parts.merge.results`:

```ts
for (const r of parts.merge.results) {       // merge-driven
  conflictPerSlice[r.sliceId] = r.conflictedFiles;
}
```

These two key sets can diverge: a sliceId present in `merge.results` but absent from `agents` will appear in `textualConflicts.perSlice` but not in `perSlice`. Conversely, a gate entry for a sliceId not in `agents` is silently dropped ‚Äî its verdict never appears anywhere in the output. In the symmetric case (an agent with no merge entry), the fallbacks `mr?.merged ?? false` and `mr?.conflictedFiles ?? []` are safe conservative defaults.

**Why it matters:** If the upstream runner ever emits mismatched inputs (e.g., a gate failure for a slice whose agent crashed before registering), the gate verdict disappears from the report while `gate.allPass` might still be false ‚Äî the overall flag fires but the per-slice attribution is gone. No test covers asymmetric inputs.

**Severity:** Important, not Critical. In the expected operating case all three sets have the same sliceIds. Flag it, don't block on it.

### Minor ‚Äî `report.ts:531`: `Math.round(parts.wallMs)`

`wallMs` is typed as `number` and the callers in tests pass integers. Rounding a value that's already an integer is harmless but signals the type should perhaps be `number` (float). If it's always integer milliseconds, `Math.round` is unnecessary noise. Not a bug.

### Minor ‚Äî `report.ts:521`: emoji in summary line

`"all-pass ‚úì"` and `"FAIL ‚úó"` ‚Äî the MEMORY file notes JB's style tends toward terse and plain. The brief says "human summary"; it doesn't prohibit emoji, but the `‚öÝ` on the SEMANTIC CONFLICT line (which the test checks by exact substring) could break if character encoding varies. The test passes as written so this is not a current defect ‚Äî just worth noting.

---

## 3. Cannot Verify from Diff

- **`AgentResult`, `MergeResult`, `GateResult` type shapes** ‚Äî the join correctness depends on the actual field names in those interfaces (e.g., `mergedCleanly` on `MergeResult`, `perSlice` on `GateResult`, `didNotComplete`/`error` on `AgentResult`). The diff imports them by reference. The implementer says "All types imported directly from their source files (no guessing)" ‚Äî but structural correctness of the import targets is not verifiable from this diff alone.
- **Whether `benchmark/runner/out/` was already present or created by tests** ‚Äî if the `out/` directory existed before this commit, it may not be fully git-ignored until `.gitignore` takes effect. Not visible from the diff.

---

**Bottom line:** Spec fully satisfied. The one code-quality finding (asymmetric join keys between `perSlice` and `textualConflicts.perSlice`) is real but low-risk in the expected use case. No blocking issues.</result>
